title: Large Wooden Milk Crate
summary: A parameterized cedar milk crate — two pickets and a furring strip, with the three dimensions each controlled by one cut so you can size it to whatever it needs to hold.
description:
Wooden crates are the potato chips of woodworking — nobody builds one — and this tutorial is written to enable the habit: two cedar fence pickets, one 1x2, brads and glue. What makes it a plan rather than just a box is that it's parameterized, and it tells you which cut controls what: the bottom 1x2s set the DEPTH (cut 1" less than desired), the vertical 1x2s set the HEIGHT (3/4" less than desired), and the front/back pickets set the WIDTH (cut to exactly what you want).

As drawn it's sized so two picket widths make the height — a proportion that happens to look right and wastes nothing. The structure is an inner 1x2 frame that the pickets clad, inside and out at the corners, so the finished crate has that double-walled milk-crate heft.

Build one for the firewood, and accept what follows: the mudroom shoes, the garden harvest, the record collection. Sand the handles-to-be edges well — a crate gets carried by whatever edge is nearest.

tools.note: Or a miter saw — every cut is a straight crosscut.
1" and 1-1/4" brads do the whole crate.
Crates get carried by their edges — soften all of them.

cutList.note: DEPTH control: cut 1" less than the crate depth you want.
HEIGHT control: cut 3/4" less than desired height. As drawn, two picket widths tall.
WIDTH control: cut to the exact crate width you want.

steps.title: Build the two inner frames
steps.body: Nail and glue the vertical 1x2 legs to the bottom 1x2s with 1-1/4" brads — two identical H-frames. These are the crate's skeleton, and the parameter cheat sheet lives here: bottom 1x2 = depth minus 1", verticals = height minus 3/4".

steps.title: Clad the sides
steps.body: Cut the side pickets and nail them across the two frames with glue — the frames disappear inside, and the crate takes shape.

steps.title: Close the front and back
steps.body: Cut the front and back pickets to your target width and attach with the 1" brads into the frame verticals. This is the cut that sets the crate's finished width — measure the thing it needs to hold, not the plan.

steps.title: Fill the inside corners
steps.body: Nail the remaining short 1x2 pieces inside, filling between the frame verticals so every corner reads as a solid post from within.

steps.title: Floor it and sand it
steps.body:
Drop the bottom pickets onto the 1x2 bottom frame and nail in place — small gaps are fine and drain whatever the crate ends up holding.

Sand every edge like it's a handle, because it is. Then build the second crate, because there's never been a single wooden crate in any house.

images.alt: Large handmade cedar milk crate with slatted sides
